full_prompt: Design the monocoque chassis of an Open-class model-solar-car benchmark. The complete car including wheels and PV array must fit within a rectangular envelope of 650 mm length x 320 mm width, with no explicit height limit but total mass target of 1.2 kg maximum for competitive performance. The top surface of the chassis must present a flat mounting area of at least 180 mm x 260 mm for an eight-cell silicon PV array. Material is carbon-fiber/epoxy composite laminate (quasi-isotropic layup, E1 = E2 = 50 GPa effective, Poisson ratio 0.30, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile strength 600 MPa, compressive strength 450 MPa) with a skin thickness of 0.8 mm and a foam core (Rohacell 51IG, density 52 kg/m^3, shear strength 0.8 MPa) of thickness 6 mm between skins. Four wheels are mounted at axle bosses located 60 mm inboard of each corner; the front axle is steerable and the rear is a fixed dead axle. Load cases for pass/fail: (LC1) static 4x safety factor on mass load: apply 4 g vertical downward body load to the full car; (LC2) cornering: 1.5 g lateral at the PV pad centroid while the outer two wheels are pinned, representing figure-8 corner entry at ~3 m/s on the 100 m course; (LC3) PV array preload: 5 N uniform download across the 180 x 260 mm array footprint representing the light-box test platen plus array mass; (LC4) drop test: 0.3 m vertical drop onto a flat surface landing on all four wheels, modeled as a 10 g impulse on the axle bosses. Pass/fail: under LC1-LC3 static, maximum von Mises stress in either composite skin must remain below 400 MPa, core shear must remain below 0.53 MPa (0.8 / 1.5), PV-pad out-of-plane deflection must remain below 2 mm, and first torsional mode must exceed 60 Hz. Under LC4 drop, no failure of skin or core is permitted (peak stress limits of LC1 scaled by 2x kept). Deliverables: STEP + composite layup table, FEA report per case, modal and drop summary, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the monocoque chassis of an Open-class model-solar car. The chassis is a sandwich composite with CFRP skins and a Rohacell foam core, hosting four wheels (front steerable, rear dead axle) and providing a flat top pad for an 8-cell silicon PV array.

No external standard is cited; design to a 1.5 FoS on skin tensile/compressive strength and on core shear.

Envelope: 650 mm x 320 mm planform, PV pad at least 260 x 180 mm, target mass <= 1.2 kg.

Load cases.
- LC1 4 g static: 4 g vertical body load on the full car.
- LC2 cornering: 1.5 g lateral at the PV-pad centroid with the outer two wheels pinned.
- LC3 light-box platen: 5 N uniform down across the 260 x 180 mm PV pad.
- LC4 drop: 0.3 m vertical drop on all four wheels, modeled as a 10 g impulse at the axle bosses.

prompt.title: Open-class model solar car chassis
prompt.brief: Sandwich composite monocoque chassis for a 650 x 320 mm model solar car.
